brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +24.1% since Feb 27 — Brent ~$88/bbl (Apr 17 pre-market, implied from -6.37% crude drop) — Apr 16 close $94.13 — peace premium unwinding — EIA STEO: Brent peaks $115/bbl Q2 2026
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 16 close ~$94.13/bbl; Apr 17 pre-market crude -6.37% to ~$85.36 WTI, Brent implied ~$88 on ceasefire headlines and China's public endorsement of peace talks. Futures cracking below $86 on Apr 17 signals investors treat a deal as the base case — creating asymmetric downside risk if Round 2 fails before Apr 22. ExxonMobil + Chevron fell ~5% Apr 16 as war premium compressed (XOM +27.6% YTD). EIA April STEO still forecasts Brent peaking at $115/bbl in Q2 2026. Blockade Day 5: 13-14 vessels turned back cumulatively; only 2 observed transits Apr 16 (below 10/day). Blockade costs Iran ~$400M/day in lost revenue; NPC suspended petrochemical exports. Hegseth (Apr 17): US forces 'locked and loaded' to resume strikes on Iran's power grid if talks fail before Apr 22. Trump (Apr 17): US-Iran deal 'very close.' Pakistan Army Chief Munir carried Washington's terms to Tehran Apr 15-16; Iran accepted third-party nuclear monitoring 'in principle'; 10-year enrichment compromise on the table. UK + France + ~40 nations Hormuz Summit Apr 17 (US excluded) — multinational alternative task force. Iran has ~13 days of oil storage per analyst estimates — coercive power peaks as Apr 22 ceasefire closes. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / EIA STEO / IEA / CENTCOM / Treasury / UN.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +28.4% since Feb 27 — WTI ~$85.36/bbl (Apr 17 pre-market -6.37%) — Apr 16 close ~$93 — gas $4.076 (AAA Apr 17, -1.7 cents from Apr 16)
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 16 close: ~$93/bbl; Apr 17 pre-market -6.37% to ~$85.36 on ceasefire headlines + China's public endorsement of peace talks. AAA national average gas: $4.076/gal (Apr 17) — down from peak $4.16 on Apr 9. California $5.89; Oklahoma $3.27. Jet fuel has roughly doubled since Feb 27 (+88%); Delta warned of $2B additional Q2 fuel costs. Diesel averages $5.43+. Treasury Sec Bessent Apr 15 forecast $3 gas this summer; designated 24+ people/companies/vessels in Shamkhani oil smuggling network Apr 16. March CPI +3.3% YoY (highest since May 2024) / +0.9% MoM (largest monthly jump since June 2022); core 2.6%. UMich preliminary April consumer sentiment 47.6 — ALL-TIME LOW in 74-year series, below June 2022 reading of 50, -10.7% from March. 1-year inflation expectations 4.8%. 10Y UST 4.311%; 2Y 3.778%; 30Y 4.937%. Fed funds 3.50-3.75%; Chicago Fed Goolsbee warned cuts could slip to 2027 if oil stays elevated. SPR releases continue: 172M bbl authorized, 53.7M awarded; new RFP Apr 9 for +30M from West Hackberry. EIA crude inventories -9.13M bbl vs +154K expected. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / Treasury / Federal Reserve / EIA.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.096 / +36.8% since Feb 27 — $4.076 nationally (AAA Apr 17) — California $5.89 — Oklahoma $3.27 — peaked $4.16 Apr 9 — Bessent: 'optimistic' about $3 gas by June-September
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.076/gal nationally (AAA Apr 17) — down from peak $4.16 on Apr 9 on peace-talk optimism but still highest level since August 2022. Most expensive state: California at $5.89; cheapest: Oklahoma at $3.27. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. Diesel national average ~$5.43+/gallon. Jet fuel +88% since Feb 27 — Delta warned of $2B additional Q2 fuel costs. Treasury Sec Bessent: 'optimistic that sometime between June 20 and September 20, we can have $3 gas again.' Treasury Apr 16 designated 24+ people, companies, and vessels in the Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani oil smuggling network; halt on 30-day waivers on Iranian/Russian oil. March CPI (Apr 10) confirmed gasoline +21.2% MoM — largest monthly spike since CPI was first published in 1967. Headline CPI 3.3% YoY. UMich April consumer sentiment 47.6 all-time low. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf taunted: 'Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' Source: AAA / BLS / Treasury / Quinnipiac / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: +144 pts / +2.1% from pre-war — S&P 7,022.95 RECORD CLOSE (Apr 15 +0.80%) — eclipses Jan 27 ATH 6,978.60 — Apr 16 intraday record 7,040.03 — VIX 18.17 — Nasdaq 11th straight up day at 24,016.02 record close
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 15 RECORD CLOSE: 7,022.95 (+0.80%) — first record close since January, eclipsing the prior Jan 27 ATH of 6,978.60. Apr 16 intraday RECORD high: 7,040.03 (+0.1-0.2%). Nasdaq Apr 15: 24,016.02 (+1.59%, record close, 11th straight up day); Apr 16 ~24,059-24,077 (+0.2%). Dow Apr 15: 48,463.72 (-0.15%); Apr 16 ~48,482 midday. Russell 2000 Apr 15: 2,713.66 (+0.30%). VIX 18.17 Apr 15 (-1.03%, lowest since Feb 26); ~18.22-18.83 Apr 16. 10Y Treasury 4.28-4.30%; 2Y 3.80%; DXY 98.06 6-wk low. Gold spot ~$4,807-4,823 Apr 15 (-0.7%); ~$4,814-4,837 Apr 16, still ~10% below pre-war peak. CVX +1.58% top Dow gainer midday Apr 16; BA -1.99% laggard. BAC +1.8% (EPS $1.11 vs $1.00); MS +4.5%; MSFT +4%; TSLA +7-8%; ORCL +4.2%; ServiceNow +7.3%. Physical-paper oil dislocation persists: dated Brent $131.97 vs futures ~$96 = $35 physical premium = scarcity flag. EIA April STEO: Brent peaks $115/bbl Q2 2026. Markets read the lull as peace; the IRGC's three-sea declaration says risk has been suppressed, not dissolved. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Bloomberg / BofA / Morgan Stanley / EIA / Federal Reserve.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — BLOCKADE DAY 4 — 13-14 vessels turned back in 72 hrs — USS Spruance interdicted Iranian-flagged vessel — 10,000+ personnel + dozens of warships and aircraft — Hegseth: 'locked and loaded'
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 48: ~$800M/day — burn rate moderated as active strike operations remain paused during ceasefire (now ~216 hours). Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes (CNBC Apr 14): ~$2B/day active fighting — 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' including long-term costs. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40-95B direct cost, up to $210B total economic impact. THAAD interceptors cost $12.7M each versus Iran's Shahed drones at $35,000 each — 363:1 ratio. CENTCOM Apr 16 reports >10,000 personnel enforcing blockade with 'more than a dozen warships and dozens of aircraft.' Roughly 300+ US aircraft already in CENTCOM (84 F/A-18E/F, 36 F-15E, 54 F-16, 42 F-35A/C, 18 EA-18G, 12 A-10C, 86 KC-46/-135 tankers, plus Patriot/THAAD). 24 F-22s at Ovda AB, Israel. 82nd Airborne IRF (~2,000 paratroopers) forward-deployed. Naval disposition (USNI Apr 13): 291 battle force ships (232 USS + 59 USNS); 105 deployed; 86 underway. Carrier groups: Lincoln CSG (Arabian Sea/Gulf of Oman ~124nm off southern Iran), Ford CSG (Eastern Med, post-Vietnam deployment record), Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (Arabian Sea). USS Spruance (DDG-111) turned back Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Apr 14 — 10th interception. Hegseth (Apr 16): 'locked and loaded' to restart combat if talks collapse. WSJ: Trump weighing resumption of limited military strikes to break Islamabad stalemate. UK explicitly told BBC it will not assist the blockade — contradicting Trump's Apr 15 Fox claim. France/UK building separate ~15-country freedom-of-navigation coalition under UN framework. Source: Pentagon / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker / Stars and Stripes / BBC / Hegseth.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (48 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$54.15B through Day 48 — Iran-cost-ticker ~$52-53B — Harvard Bilmes: $1T long-term certain — Penn Wharton: up to $210B total economic impact — Senate WPR fails 47-52 (4th time); May 1 War Powers deadline + Apr 21-22 ceasefire expiry = two binding clocks
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Internal site tally: ~$54.15B (Day 48). External estimates: Iran-cost-ticker.com ~$52-53B; AEI ~$50B total economic impact incl. fuel costs; Penn Wharton Budget Model $40-95B direct, up to $210B total economic impact; Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes (CNBC Apr 14): ~$2B/day active fighting, 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' long-term/lifecycle. Ceasefire reduces burn rate but blockade ops add costs. Pentagon $200B supplemental remains pending. Senate REJECTED War Powers Resolution 47-52 on Apr 15 — fourth defeat of the Kaine resolution; Rand Paul (R-KY) sole GOP yea, Fetterman (D-PA) sole Dem nay; Blumenthal pledged to refile. Thune said leadership 'feel pretty good' about military gains but needs 'plan to wind this down'; Hawley wants 60-day AUMF vote; Curtis won't back further war funding without authorization. 60-day War Powers Act deadline falls ~May 1, setting up next inflection with appropriations. Cost to Iran (Iran govt): ~$270B damages. FDD: blockade costs Iran ~$435M/day. IMF Apr 15 cut 2026 MENA growth forecast to 1.1% (from 3.9%) and flagged global recession risk. Quinnipiac (Apr 15): Trump 38% approve / 55% disapprove; 65% blame Trump for gas prices; 64-28% reject Trump's 'whole civilization will die' framing. CNN poll: just 34% approve military action (-7 pts), 25% call war 'strategic success,' 71% oppose $200B supplemental, 68% oppose ground troops. Reuters/Ipsos Trump 36%; Fox 59% disapprove (record). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard Bilmes / Penn Wharton / Senate / IMF / Quinnipiac / CNN / Reuters/Ipsos.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ON IRAN FRONT HOLDS ~216 HRS — 35,500+ cumulative — zero kinetic US-Iran action in 48 hrs — IDF 98th Division consolidates Bint Jbeil (100+ Hezbollah killed) — IDF Nabatieh strike kills 19 incl. 13 Lebanese State Security
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. Cumulative: 900+ strikes in first 12 hours (Feb 28); 1,879+ US-Israeli strikes documented by ACLED through mid-March across 29 of 31 provinces. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~216 hours, ceasefire technically holds). Hegseth (Pentagon Apr 16): US 'locked and loaded' to restart combat if talks collapse; WSJ reports Trump weighing resumption of limited strikes to break Islamabad stalemate. CNN Apr 14 satellite imagery shows Iran ACTIVELY RECONSTITUTING missile capabilities during the ceasefire — equipment, dump trucks, front-end loaders visible at blocked tunnel entrances near underground missile bases at Khomeyn and south of Tabriz; US intel assesses ~50% of Iran's missile launchers survived the first 40 days. Lebanon front intensifying: IDF strike on Nabatieh Apr 16 killed 19 people including 13 Lebanese State Security personnel; last functioning hospital in Tebnin reportedly struck Apr 15; IDF 98th Division consolidated Bint Jbeil where 100+ Hezbollah fighters have been killed cumulatively and 3 surrendered Apr 15. IDF Chief Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir called southern Lebanon a 'kill zone.' 5 IDF divisions operating (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd). Operation 'Silver Plough' demolishing border village structures. ACLED: ≥70 fatalities from 560+ outbound Iranian attacks across 14 countries; 619+ interceptions; 163 energy-sector events. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI / CNN / WSJ / ACLED / Haaretz / AFP.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 1 non-combat death — 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries — no new US casualties in 216 hrs of ceasefire — blockade Day 4 still not kinetically tested
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM official: 13 killed in hostile action plus 1 non-combat death (Maj. Sorffly Davius, Kuwait), 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8). Breakdown: 7 by direct enemy fire, 6 in KC-46/KC-135 crash in Iraq Mar 13. The Intercept disputes: at least 15 KIA and 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire casualties (~200 sailors), F-15 shoot-down injuries, and A-10 crash near Hormuz excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7, ~216 hours). Blockade Day 4 (Apr 16): 13-14 vessels turned back in 72 hours; USS Spruance (DDG-111) turned back an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in Gulf of Oman Apr 14; 10,000+ personnel enforcing blockade with dozens of warships/aircraft. No mine incidents, no missile launches at US vessels, no Iranian tanker seizures. IRGC warning: any military vessel approaching Strait triggers 'harsh and decisive' force. Mojtaba Khamenei 'military adviser' warned Iran would 'sink American ships in the Strait of Hormuz.' Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept / CSIS / Stars & Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire sailors (~200), F-15 shoot-down, A-10 crash — DCAS internal data reportedly 23% above CENTCOM — ceasefire ~216 hrs
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM official: 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8) — 344 returned to duty, 5 seriously wounded. The Intercept disputes: at least 520+ total injuries including 200+ USS Ford fire sailors, F-15 shoot-down injuries, and A-10 crash near Hormuz excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~216 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Blockade Day 4 (Apr 16): 13-14 vessels turned back in 72 hours; 10,000+ personnel + dozens of warships and aircraft deployed. IRGC warning: any military vessel approaching Strait triggers 'harsh and decisive' force. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ FROZEN SINCE APR 7 / HRANA: 3,636 (1,701 civ, 1,221 mil) / Iran Intl: 4,700+ security / Hengaw: 6,620 military (Apr 8) / Red Crescent: ~2,000/27,000 injuries — ceasefire ~216 hrs — MSNBC regional aggregate 'nearly 6,000'
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — deeply contested across sources with a nearly 4x spread, and Iran MoH reporting FROZEN since April 7 (reporting suppression). Iran Health Ministry (official): 2,076+ killed (static since Apr 7); Iran Red Crescent (Apr 10): ~2,000 fatalities, ~27,000 injuries (incl. 4,000 women, 1,621 children). HRANA (US-based NGO, Apr 7): 3,636 total (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified). Iran International (Mar 31): 4,700+ security forces killed. Hengaw (Norway-based, Apr 8): 6,620 military killed. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed, 15,000 wounded (contested). Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. MSNBC aggregate (Apr 15): regional total 'nearly 6,000.' ACLED (Apr 15): ≥70 fatalities abroad from 560+ outbound Iranian attacks across 14 countries; 619+ interceptions; 163 energy-sector events. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7 (~216 hours). Trump's Apr 14 Truth Social claim of 42,000 protesters killed is unsupported (HRANA ~7,000, Iran govt 3,100+) — flagged as unverified. CNN Apr 14 satellite imagery shows Iran ACTIVELY RECONSTITUTING missile capabilities during the ceasefire; US intel: ~50% launchers survived first 40 days. Up to 3.2M IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12); 4.4M Afghan refugees at elevated risk; 70,000+ returns to Afghanistan by mid-March. 81,000+ civilian units damaged per Iran Red Crescent (61K homes, 19K commercial, 481 schools, 30+ universities, 120+ heritage sites). WHO verified 13+ health sites struck by Mar 5. All Iranian ports blockaded since Apr 13. Source: Iran Health Ministry / Iran Red Crescent / HRANA / Iran International / Hengaw / IDF / OCHA / WHO / CNN / UNHCR / ACLED / MSNBC.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 2,167 killed / 7,061 wounded (Lebanese MoPH Apr 15) — +35 since Apr 15 — Apr 16 IDF strike on Nabatieh kills 19 incl. 13 State Security — last Tebnin hospital reportedly hit Apr 15 — IDF Chief Zamir: south Lebanon a 'kill zone' — Trump floats unconfirmed 10-day ceasefire
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative end Apr 15 (Lebanese MoPH via WAFA/Al Jazeera): 2,167 killed / 7,061 wounded, ≥166 children, 250+ women, 51-53 health workers killed. UNICEF: 600+ children killed or injured since March 2. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' single-day toll documented at 357 killed (50 jets, ~160 bombs, 100 targets in 10 minutes). IDF forced evacuation orders cover ~14% of Lebanon's territory (1,470 sq km). Displacement: >1.2M (~20% of population); >200,000 Lebanese/Syrians crossed into Syria Mar 2-27; 140,682 in 684 collective shelters (Apr 12). Apr 15 events: IDF airstrikes hit vehicle in Jiyeh (south of Beirut) and Qlaileh near Tyre; IDF 98th Division consolidated Bint Jbeil — 100+ Hezbollah killed cumulatively, 3 surrendered Apr 15; last functioning hospital in Tebnin reportedly struck; Hezbollah Apr 15 rockets wounded 5 IDF soldiers (1 serious, 4 light). Apr 16: IDF strike on Nabatieh killed 19 people including 13 Lebanese State Security personnel; Hezbollah struck Ashdod (claimed Ashdod Naval Base); missile damaged 1,500-year-old church in Nahariya. Cumulative Hezbollah rocket/UAV activity since Mar 2: 1,121 attack waves (Alma Center). IDF Chief Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir called south Lebanon a 'kill zone.' Israeli security cabinet met Apr 15 to debate a proposed 1-week Lebanon ceasefire — ended without decision. Trump floated an unconfirmed 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Apr 16 (NBC). Hezbollah MP Hussein Hajj Hassan condemned the Apr 14 Israel-Lebanon talks as 'a grave sin.' Rubio-Aoun phone call Apr 16 on Lebanon ceasefire. Lebanon Flash Appeal $308.3M, 30.6% funded. WHO: 92-133 attacks on healthcare, 51-53 health workers killed / 91-137 injured. 19 UN Human Rights experts Apr 15 called Israel's Apr 8 strikes a 'blatant violation of UN Charter.' Source: Lebanese MoPH / WAFA / Al Jazeera / IDF / UNICEF / NBC / WHO / UNHCR / Haaretz / Alma Center / OCHA.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4M+ regional — Iran 3.2M+ IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12, likely stale) — Lebanon >1.2M (140,682 in 684 shelters OCHA Apr 12) — 200,000+ Lebanese/Syrians crossed into Syria Mar 2-27 — WHO EMRO: 1 in 7 in 750M-person region needs aid
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Regional total: 4 million+. Iran: 3.2 million+ internally displaced (UNHCR Mar 12 — figure likely stale), compounded by severe flooding in 24 provinces Mar 25-30 + additional flooding Apr 5 in Rudbar/Gilan. 4.4M Afghans in Iran at elevated risk; 70,000+ returns to Afghanistan by mid-March. Lebanon internal: >1.2 million displaced (~20% of population, IOM/Lebanon disaster mgmt); 140,682 in 684 collective shelters (OCHA Apr 12); >200,000 Lebanese/Syrians crossed into Syria Mar 2-27. ICRC delivered its first medical shipment into Iran Apr 12 — first since war start. WHO Lebanon: 60+ tonnes of medical supplies mobilized since Mar 18 via EU Humanitarian Airbridge. Lebanon Flash Appeal: $308.3M requested, 30.6% funded. WHO Flash Appeal: $30M for 5 most-affected countries (including $10M for Lebanon). UNICEF reached 188,155 IDPs in 360+ shelters (Apr 1). Airspace closures continue disrupting WHO Dubai hub. WHO EMRO Director Balkhy (Apr 14): '1 in every 7' people in the 750M-person EMR region now need humanitarian assistance. No confirmed major outbreak; insulin stockout reported in Lebanese primary care; respiratory/eye risks from refinery strikes. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / IOM / UNICEF / IFRC / ICRC.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — BLOCKADE DAY 4: 13-14 vessels turned back in 72 hrs — USS Spruance interdicted Iranian-flagged vessel — BBC Verify disputes CENTCOM 'zero penetrations' — Agios Fanourios I first crude carrier west post-blockade
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz blockade Day 4 (Apr 16 CENTCOM 72-hr tally): 13-14 vessels turned back (6 first 24 hrs → 9 in 48 hrs → 13-14 in 72 hrs). USS Spruance (DDG-111) turned back Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in Gulf of Oman Apr 14 (Stars & Stripes). Adm. Brad Cooper: blockade 'fully implemented.' Hegseth (Apr 16): US 'locked and loaded' to restart combat if talks collapse. WSJ: Trump weighing resumption of limited strikes to break Islamabad stalemate. BBC Verify/MarineTraffic disputes CENTCOM's 'zero penetrations' claim — at least 4 Iran-related transits Apr 14; Chinese-flagged Rich Starry U-turned at Gulf of Oman; Elpis stopped near Iranian coast; two tankers stopped at Chabahar; the Iraq-bound supertanker Agios Fanourios I became the first crude carrier to head west post-blockade (gCaptain). Hormuz transit volume below 10% of pre-war average. UK explicitly told BBC it will not assist the blockade — contradicting Trump's Apr 15 Fox claim that UK + 'a couple of other countries' are sending minesweepers. France/UK building separate ~15-country freedom-of-navigation coalition under UN framework. China MFA called blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible'; Lavrov: 'no military solution.' IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi declared Apr 15 three-sea counter-blockade doctrine (Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea); Iran's National Petrochemical Co suspended all petrochemical exports 'until further notice.' Treasury Apr 15: secondary sanctions — 3 individuals, 17 entities, 9 tankers tied to Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani; waivers halted on Iranian/Russian oil. IEA April OMR: 10.1 mb/d March loss — largest in history; Hormuz 3.8 mb/d (80%+ decline). Blockade costs Iran ~$435M/day (FDD). Source: CENTCOM / WSJ / Reuters / Kpler / IEA / BBC Verify / Stars & Stripes / gCaptain / Treasury / FDD.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran MoH: 212+ children (reporting frozen) — HRANA Iran: 15% of 3,636 — UNICEF Lebanon: 600+ killed or injured since Mar 2 — ≥166 Lebanese children killed — Pope Leo XIV on Algeria tour
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran MoH: 212+ children killed (reporting frozen since Apr 7); HRANA: ~15% of 3,636 total. Iran Red Crescent (Apr 10): 1,621 children among wounded. UNICEF reports 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since March 2; ≥166 Lebanese children killed cumulatively (Lebanese MoPH Apr 15). Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' toll: 357 killed including ~33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). 763 schools damaged in Iran, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. Pope Leo XIV remains in Algeria (Apr 13-14 trip); his Apr 11 St. Peter's vigil ('Enough of war!') remains his most recent major intervention. No fresh Vatican statement Apr 15-16. Trump attacked Pope Leo XIV again on Truth Social Apr 14-15 (in the '42,000 protesters' post); VP Vance told a rally the Pope should 'be careful' on 'matters of theology'; Cardinal McElroy: Iran war fails Catholic just war criteria. Iran executions during war include teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi. Source: UNICEF / Iran MoH / Iran Red Crescent / HRANA / Lebanese MoPH / WHO / HRW / Vatican / Hengaw.
